title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs complexity, timing, profiling, memory, and capacity from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for correctness-preserving capacity study after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.
Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Performance Regression, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Performance Regression precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Performance Regression, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 7522 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Performance Regression, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A faster result may be accepted even if Unicode or ranking output changes.
